title: excoecaria obtusa
intro: Excoecaria obtusa is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, known for its toxic leaves, which contain a compound called phorbol ester, making it a potential source of medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues due to its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Health benefits associated with Excoecaria obtusa include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and provide antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, or decoctions, although it is essential to exercise caution when handling the plant due to its toxicity.
